content.problemStatement:
You are a teacher at Berland School. There are NNN students in your class who are standing in a queue. The queue looks **good** if students are standing in ascending order of their height. In one move you can ask any two students to swap their positions if those two students are standing adjacent to each other. You also want to give minimum inconvenience to students, so you have to do the rearrangement in a minimum number of moves.

The heights of the students is expressed can be represented in the form of any array. The height of the student standing at ithi^{th}ith position is AiA_iAi​.

Determine the minimum number of moves needed to make the queue look good, also determine the moves you make. It is guaranteed that you can make the queue good by using at most 10510^5105 moves.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each test case will contain two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains N integers, A1,A2...ANA_1, A_2 . . . A_NA1​,A2​...AN​.


Output Format

In a single line, output a single integer KKK, denoting the minimum number of moves needed to make the queue look good.
Print two integers iii and jjj, in the following KKK lines, denoting the position of students whose positions you wish to swap.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the sum of NNN over all the test cases is less than or equal to 3∗1053*10^53∗105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test-Case:1
Let’s see how array looks after each move
After 1st move ( i.e. swapping 2nd and 3rd element of array )
1 4 5 8 3
After 2nd move ( i.e. swapping 4th and 5th element of array )
1 4 5 3 8
After 3rd move ( i.e. swapping 3rd and 4th element of array )
1 4 3 5 8
After 4th move ( i.e. swapping 2nd and 3rd element of array )
1 3 4 5 8
